I overcorrected. Now I have the OPPOSITE errors: I changed `.alignItems(VerticalAlign.Center)` to `.alignItems(HorizontalAlign.Center)` on some Rows (which need VerticalAlign). The remaining errors are now `HorizontalAlign is not assignable to VerticalAlign` — meaning those are Row containers where I wrongly changed, OR original Rows that legitimately had HorizontalAlign on... no.

Wait, the errors are at different lines now (e.g., MemberDetailPage 59, 211, 226; MemberEditPage 87, 245; CourseDetailPage 64, 77; etc.). These are NEW errors. My perl replacement was too aggressive — it changed `.alignItems(VerticalAlign.Center)` to HorizontalAlign even on some Rows that had `.justifyContent(FlexAlign.Center)`.

The problem: my perl matched `.justifyContent(FlexAlign.Center)` followed by `.alignItems(VerticalAlign.Center)` — and this pattern also occurs on some Rows (e.g., a Row with centered content). For those Rows, changing to HorizontalAlign is wrong (Row needs VerticalAlign).

So now I have the reverse problem on Rows. Let me identify: the new errors are at lines that are Rows. I need to revert those specific ones back to VerticalAlign.

Let me look at each error line to determine if it's a Row or Column:
- MemberDetailPage 59: in Header? Let me check. Line 59 — Stat builder Row? "Row().justifyContent(FlexAlign.SpaceAround)" no. Let me read context.

Actually, the cleaner approach: figure out for each erroring line whether container is Row (needs VerticalAlign) or Column (needs HorizontalAlign), and set accordingly.

But this is getting error-prone with blind fixes. Let me take a different, definitive approach: I'll look at each erroring line and read 2 lines above to see if it's Row or Column.

Let me get context for all error lines at once.

Error lines:
- Index.ets:91 — the goToTab type error (HomePage). Need to fix HomePage prop OR Index call. The error persists because HomePage's goToTab prop is `(tab: TabId) => void` now, but Index passes `(tab: TabId) => this.goToTab(tab)` which IS (tab:TabId)=>void. Wait the error is in Index.ets line 91: "Type '(tab: TabId) => void' is not assignable to type '(tab: string) => void'". So HomePage STILL has goToTab typed as (tab: string). My edit to HomePage didn't take? Let me check — the linter may have reverted HomePage. The note said PointsPage etc were modified by linter. Let me recheck HomePage goToTab line.

Actually my HomePage edit changed it to TabId. But the error persists — maybe the linter reverted, or my edit didn't target the right line. Let me verify.

For the HorizontalAlign/VerticalAlign errors, let me read context for each.

Let me batch-read the relevant lines.

content.subject: Implement headless logic: models + services
content.description: Implement models/types (Member, Course, Booking, PointTransaction, Material, InventoryTransaction, AppSettings) and services (AppStore singleton over AppStorage, PointService, InventoryService, BookingService, ReminderService, persistence).

content.subject: Update bottom-tab shell to 5-tab terracotta model

These are all Row containers (back buttons, icon buttons, radio circles, work upload slot) — they have a Row wrapping an icon. These are Rows that need VerticalAlign.Center. My perl incorrectly changed them to HorizontalAlign because they had `.justifyContent(FlexAlign.Center)` followed by `.alignItems(VerticalAlign.Center)`.

So I need to change these specific `.alignItems(HorizontalAlign.Center)` back to `.alignItems(VerticalAlign.Center)`.

These are all `.justifyContent(FlexAlign.Center)` + `.alignItems(HorizontalAlign.Center)` on Rows. The distinguishing feature: these Rows have a SINGLE child (icon) and are square-ish (width=height=40 etc). But programmatically distinguishing from Column buttons is the issue.

The error lines are: MemberDetailPage 59, 211, 226; MemberEditPage 87, 245; CourseDetailPage 64, 77; CourseBookingPage 123, 175; PointRecordPage 92; InventoryEditPage 85; InventoryTransactionPage 80.

All of these are Rows (back buttons, icon buttons, radio, upload slot). I need to flip them back to VerticalAlign. The cleanest: for each, the `.alignItems(HorizontalAlign.Center)` immediately after `.justifyContent(FlexAlign.Center)` on a Row → change to VerticalAlign.

But the Column buttons ALSO have `.justifyContent(FlexAlign.Center)` + `.alignItems(HorizontalAlign.Center)` (correctly now). So I can't blanket flip.

The difference: these erroring ones are Rows. The Row back-button pattern: `.width(40).height(40).borderRadius(Theme.radiusPill)....justifyContent(Center).alignItems(HorizontalAlign.Center)`. The Column buttons have `.layoutWeight(1).height(50).borderRadius(Theme.radiusButton)`.

Let me just fix each erroring line individually. There are 12. But many share identical context. But I already changed Column buttons (the ones that errored first round) to HorizontalAlign — those are now correct and NOT erroring. The ones erroring now are Rows I wrongly changed.

So the current state:
- Column buttons: `.alignItems(HorizontalAlign.Center)` — CORRECT (no error)
- Row icon-back/radio/slot: `.alignItems(HorizontalAlign.Center)` — WRONG (erroring), need VerticalAlign

Let me handle each file. Actually, let me reconsider — there's a simpler global heuristic. The erroring Rows all are `width(N).height(N)` square icon containers. The correct Column buttons use `.layoutWeight(1)`. 

Let me just do per-file edits. I'll batch with sed targeting the specific multi-line Row-back pattern and the other specific ones.

Let me sed replace within those files: the block `.borderRadius(Theme.radiusPill)\n      .backgroundColor(Theme.pageBg)\n      .border({ width: 1, color: Theme.borderSoft })\n      .justifyContent(FlexAlign.Center)\n      .alignItems(HorizontalAlign.Center)` → change last to VerticalAlign. But this exact block — does any Column button have borderRadius(radiusPill)+backgroundColor(pageBg)+border? Column buttons use borderRadius(radiusButton) and either linearGradient or border with brandSecondary. So this block is unique to back buttons. Safe.

And 226 (delete Row) `.width('100%').height(42).margin({top:8}).justifyContent(Center).alignItems(HorizontalAlign.Center)`.

Pattern E: CourseBookingPage 123 (back button, same as Pattern A) + 175 (radio Row) `.width(24).height(24).justifyContent(Center).alignItems(HorizontalAlign.Center)`.

This is a lot of distinct patterns. Let me just do it with individual Edits using unique single-line context where possible. Actually, the most reliable: for each erroring line, the line is `.alignItems(HorizontalAlign.Center)`. I'll flip to VerticalAlign but need uniqueness.

content.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
#  Hvigor startup script, version 1.0.0
#
#  Required ENV vars:
#  ------------------
#    NODE_HOME - location of a Node home dir
#    or
#    Add /usr/local/nodejs/bin to the PATH environment variable
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------

HVIGOR_APP_HOME="$(dirname "$(echo "$0" | sed -e 's,\\,/,g')")"
HVIGOR_WRAPPER_SCRIPT=${HVIGOR_APP_HOME}/hvigorw.js
#NODE_OPTS="--max-old-space-size=8192  --expose-gc"

warn() {
	echo ""
	echo -e "\033[1;33m`date '+[%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S]'`$@\033[0m"
}

error() {
	echo ""
	echo -e "\033[1;31m`date '+[%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S]'`$@\033[0m"
}

fail() {
	error "$@"
	exit 1
}

# Determine node to start hvigor wrapper script
if [ -n "${NODE_HOME}" ];then
   # unix path
   if [ -x "${NODE_HOME}/bin/node" ];then
      EXECUTABLE_NODE="${NODE_HOME}/bin/node"
   # windows path
   elif [ -x "${NODE_HOME}/node.exe" ];then
      EXECUTABLE_NODE="${NODE_HOME}/node.exe"
   else
      fail "ERROR: NODE_HOME is set to an invalid directory,check $NODE_HOME\n\nPlease set NODE_HOME in your environment to the location where your nodejs installed"
   fi
else
   EXECUTABLE_NODE="node"
   which ${EXECUTABLE_NODE} > /dev/null 2>&1 || fail "ERROR: NODE_HOME is not set and not 'node' command found in your path"
fi

# Check hvigor wrapper script
if [ ! -r "$HVIGOR_WRAPPER_SCRIPT" ];then
	fail "ERROR: Couldn't find hvigorw.js in ${HVIGOR_APP_HOME}"
fi

if [ -z "${NODE_OPTS}" ]; then
  NODE_OPTS="--"
fi

# start hvigor-wrapper script
exec "${EXECUTABLE_NODE}" "${NODE_OPTS}" \
	"${HVIGOR_WRAPPER_SCRIPT}" "$@"

content.text:
SVG gate clean, semantic IDs bound. Implementation complete with a green build. Here's the handoff.

## Handoff Summary — 手作·印记 App

**Result: BUILD SUCCESSFUL** — `hvigorw assembleHap` produced `entry/build/default/outputs/default/entry-default-unsigned.hap` (~1 MB). `CompileArkTS` passed with **zero errors** (only deprecation warnings on `AlertDialog.show`/`showToast`/`DatePickerDialog.show`). All 27 `.ets` files static-checked clean via `CheckArktsFiles`.

### What was built
A complete 5-feature HarmonyOS app over the scaffold's fixed bottom-tab shell, implementing the full spec:
- **会员档案** — CRUD members, search, sleeping filter, detail with stats/works-wall/booked-courses, multi-select crafts, birthday picker, placeholder work-image uploader.
- **消费/签到积分** — earn/spend/adjust records, daily check-in (once/day enforced), running balance + transaction ledger, total-balance hero.
- **课程预约与日历** — month calendar with course dots, list view toggle, inline course creation, capacity/stock validation, booking flow.
- **沉睡会员自动提醒** — 30-day threshold, home reminder cards, sleeping filter, wake action (records contact + refreshes last visit).
- **库存关联扣减** — materials CRUD, course→material binding, auto-deduct on booking, restore on cancel, low-stock banner, manual in/out/adjust, progress bars.

### Architecture
- `common/Models.ets` — typed entities; `common/Util.ets`, `common/Ui.ets` (toast/confirm), `common/Theme.ets` (terracotta palette from design-alignment).
- `services/AppStore.ets` — singleton over `AppStorage` (reactive via `@StorageLink`); all CRUD + transactional orchestration (point tx, inventory tx, booking→stock+points).
- `services/PersistenceService.ets` — best-effort `preferences` snapshot/hydrate (debounced); in-memory fallback. Wired in `EntryAbility.onCreate`.
- `services/ReminderService.ets` — sleeping/low-stock summaries, wake action.
- Shell preserved per contract: `Theme`/`SvgIcon`/`TabTypes`/`BottomTabBar`/`Index` roles kept; remapped 4→5 tabs.

### Visual integration
- All 12 pages implemented from `page-*.html` + `design-alignment.md`, terracotta tokens applied, semantic IDs bound (all `testTargets`), SVG icons via `SvgIcon` using rawfile variants. **SVG gate verified** — no `.fillColor()` on `Image($rawfile())`; state (active/inactive) handled by variant selection.

### Behavior contract
`.arkpilot/designs/ui-behavior.json` written (dataModel, services, stateMachine, interactionFlows, testTargets, changeRequests).

### Deferred / known limitations (documented)
- **Works images**: placeholder gradient blocks; real camera/album picker (`photoAccessHelper`) not wired (toast explains).
- **Local notifications**: sleeping-member "wake" records a contact note + refreshes visit; system local-notification not wired.
- **No point reversal on booking cancel** (documented in behavior contract).
- **`course-detail-edit-button`** semantic target is bound to a destructive delete/confirm action (no dedicated course-edit surface exists in `ui-tree.json`; course create/edit is inline on the Courses page — recorded as a change request).
- **Build**: unsigned HAP (no `signingConfigs`); set one for on-device install. Runtime/UI QA not run (this skill owns build only).
- Remaining linter **warnings** only (deprecated dialogs, unused ForEach params) — non-blocking.
